message:
fix(ondisk): correct off-by-one in ext4 inode extended field parsing

The condition guarding access to extended inode fields (extra_isize,
crtime, projid, etc.) used a strict greater-than check (len > 0x82)
when it should have been greater-than-or-equal (len >= 0x82). This
caused inodes whose buffer was exactly 130 bytes — the minimum size
that contains the extra_isize field at offset 0x80 — to fall through
to the else branch and report no extended fields. This meant creation
timestamps, project IDs, and other extended attributes were silently
dropped for inodes at the boundary size, which is common in ext4
filesystems with 128-byte base inodes and a 2-byte extra_isize header.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
